claim: The Bible contains approximately 2,350 verses that talk about money and possessions
11 of 39 parables mention money; 15% of Jesus's teaching concerns wealth
The Galilean economy was not uniformly poor; local agents led economic activities alongside elites
Jesus worked as a tekton (carpenter/builder), a skilled tradesman, not destitute
Christian minimalism is about focusing on loving God and neighbor and intentionally removing everything else
Minimalism offers a 'slender salvation' compared to the gospel - freedom from stuff but not from sin
Secular minimalism has a quasi-religious structure: fall narrative, conversion experience, and salvation
Simplicity can become a form of works-righteousness
Not to share our riches with the poor is a robbery of the poor
verified_by: John Chrysostom, On Wealth and Poverty

claim: It is not wealth itself but attachment to wealth that endangers the soul
verified_by: Clement of Alexandria, Who Is the Rich Man That Shall Be Saved?

claim: Calvinist theology - particularly predestination - drove believers to seek worldly success as a sign of election
verified_by: Max Weber, The Protestant Ethic and the Spirit of Capitalism

claim: Weber's thesis has been criticized; capitalism flourished in Catholic Italy before the Reformation
John Wesley taught: gain all you can, save all you can, give all you can
Users on r/ChristianMinimalism report decluttering as spiritual relief
Modern minimalism can be a status symbol for the elite - expensive simplicity
verified_by: Kyle Chayka, The Longing for Less
